title: Trump’s crypto ethics stance leaves Clarity Act negotiations in limbo
headline_summary: Trump's crypto ethics stance stalls Clarity Act progress
blurb: An ethics provision addressing Trump's vast crypto wealth has frozen Clarity Act negotiations. Last week's closed-door meeting yielded no public resolution or guidance, leaving regulators and lawmakers divided. For venture capital in crypto, stalled clarity on political figures' digital asset holdings creates immediate compliance uncertainty—it will reshape how portfolio companies design disclosure frameworks and manage regulatory exposure going forward.

title: Former NY Governor Cuomo joins OKX board, touts 24/7 tokenized trading
headline_summary: Former NY Governor Cuomo joins OKX board amid US expansion
blurb: Andrew Cuomo, former New York Governor, joined OKX's board of directors. The crypto exchange recruited Cuomo specifically to champion 24/7 tokenized trading and increased international capital access in US markets, leveraging his deep relationships with regulators and key policymakers. The appointment underscores OKX's strategic push into the US market and reflects the crypto industry's intensifying campaign to influence national trading policy decisions.

title: Tom Blomfield is taking a leave of absence from YC to join Anthropic
headline_summary: Tom Blomfield leaves Y Combinator to join Anthropic
blurb: Tom Blomfield is taking leave from Y Combinator to join Anthropic. Blomfield founded Wise and spent years at YC; he's now moving to the AI company as it scales toward becoming a core player in frontier AI infrastructure. The move reflects how top venture talent is consolidating around AI's highest-stakes bets, clearly signaling where the best builders see capital flowing next.

title: OpenAI power consolidates under co-founder Greg Brockman ahead of prospective IPO
headline_summary: Greg Brockman consolidates OpenAI power as Simo departs
blurb: Greg Brockman consolidates operational authority at OpenAI as Fidji Simo departs due to a chronic medical condition. Simo held a senior product role; his exit reshuffles leadership ahead of OpenAI's expected IPO. C-suite reshuffles at pre-IPO unicorns typically precede going-public processes. This signals governance restructuring and investor positioning: the company and backers believe the executive team can handle public-market scrutiny.

title: The 7 Hires a Hardware Startup Needs to Get Right
headline_summary: a16z on the seven critical non-engineering hires hardware startups need
blurb: a16z identifies the seven non-engineering hires critical to hardware startup success. Their thesis: brilliant engineers produce strong products, yet companies systematically fail from weak business operations, inadequate go-to-market execution, insufficient leadership benches, and poor financial management. For venture investors evaluating hardware, this reframes due diligence priorities: founder competence in recruiting, building, and leading world-class business teams matters as much as technical innovation and market timing.

title: Import AI 465: Open vs closed gaps; Kimi K3; Demis' big policy plan
headline_summary: Open versus closed AI models, Kimi K3, Demis' policy shift
blurb: The AI sector diverges sharply between open-source and proprietary closed model strategies. Kimi K3 represents China's push toward competitive AI capability, while Demis Hassabis's policy agenda signals DeepMind's broader vision for AI regulation and market structure. These competing bets determine whether open models face commoditization or closed systems encounter regulatory pressure—outcomes that matter critically for venture capital's AI investment thesis.

title: EDX Markets Closes $76M Series C Led by SBI Holdings
headline_summary: EDX Markets closes $76M Series C led by SBI Holdings
blurb: EDX Markets, an institutional-only crypto trading venue with its own central clearinghouse, closed a $76 million Series C funding round. SBI Holdings, the Tokyo-listed financial group, led the round and became a strategic investor. The funding signals strong institutional appetite for regulated crypto infrastructure and reflects growing mainstream finance acceptance of professional-grade digital asset trading as a core business capability.

title: Natural, which aims to help companies let their AI agents make autonomous payments, raised a $30M Series A led by Forerunner, bringing its total raised to $40M (Marina Temkin/TechCrunch)
headline_summary: Natural raises $30M Series A for autonomous AI agent payments
blurb: Natural closed a $30M Series A led by Forerunner, bringing total funding to $40M. The platform enables AI agents to autonomously identify vendors, negotiate pricing, compare freight options, and execute payments without human approval. This reflects a critical inflection point: as AI agents move beyond analysis into autonomous business execution, they need financial authority to operate independently, making automated payment authorization infrastructure essential for enterprise deployments.

title: Chicago-based Empirical Security, which uses AI to help companies predict threats by monitoring exploited vulnerabilities, raised a $25M Series A (Chris Metinko/Axios)
headline_summary: Empirical Security raises $25M Series A from Brightmind Partners
blurb: Empirical Security closed a $25 million Series A from Brightmind Partners, led by CEO Ed Bellis. The Chicago-based exposure management company uses artificial intelligence to predict enterprise threats by monitoring and analyzing exploited vulnerabilities across customer organizations. This validates sustained investor confidence in predictive cybersecurity technology and reflects strong market demand for advanced AI-powered automated threat detection platforms.

title: Infinity, founded by Jeremy Nixon, the creator of hacker network community AGI House, to build an inference library that runs on all chips, raised a $15M seed (Dominic-Madori Davis/TechCrunch)
headline_summary: Infinity raises $15M seed for universal inference library
blurb: Infinity, founded by Jeremy Nixon (creator of AGI House), raised $15M seed at $100M valuation to build a universal inference library. The company runs AI models across any hardware chip without custom per-platform optimization, addressing a recurring infrastructure bottleneck in ML deployment. The funding signals strong VC appetite for hardware-agnostic inference middleware—infrastructure that reduces deployment friction and eliminates repetitive chip-specific optimization work.

title: Inference startup Infinity raises $15M from Touring Capital, OpenAI and Anthropic researchers
headline_summary: AI inference startup Infinity raises $15M at $100M valuation
blurb: Inference startup Infinity raised $15M at a $100M valuation, with Touring Capital and Principal VC leading and participation from researchers at OpenAI and Anthropic. The presence of AI lab researchers as individual investors reflects the talent-to-LP pipeline now increasingly common in AI infrastructure deals. The round signals sustained investor appetite for inference optimization, viewed as a remaining computational bottleneck as foundation model deployment scales globally.

title: Benchmark backs Exodus after 25% workforce cut, says stablecoin pivot is being overlooked
headline_summary: Benchmark backs Exodus stablecoin pivot after 25% workforce cut
blurb: Exodus has restructured with a 25% workforce reduction and is pivoting to stablecoins, with Benchmark remaining as a backer of the company. The restructuring is projected to generate $10 million to $13 million in annualized cost savings. The move reflects a broader shift in crypto venture investing: capital is consolidating around stablecoins as the durable play, shedding earlier bets on infrastructure and Layer-1 chains.

title: CuspAI raises €393.2 million at €2.2 billion valuation; launches AI Materials Foundry to accelerate materials discovery
headline_summary: CuspAI raises €393M Series B for AI-driven materials discovery
blurb: CuspAI announced a €393.2 million Series B funding round at a €2.2 billion valuation for its AI-powered materials discovery system. Kleiner Perkins led the round for the Cambridge-based startup, which also launched AI Materials Foundry, a global network designed to accelerate materials design cycles. The capital signals VC confidence in AI-driven materials science as a practical path to faster and cheaper compound discovery.

title: From research project to €1B-backed AI lab in 18 months: Prior Labs is Europe’s new deeptech benchmark
headline_summary: Prior Labs raises €1B backing in 18 months
blurb: Prior Labs, a European AI research lab, closed a €1B funding round. The company progressed from academic research project to billion-dollar status in just 18 months, setting a benchmark pace for European deeptech maturation. This signals that major European and international LPs are now writing billion-dollar checks for continent-born AI labs, meaningfully reshaping how venture capital flows to European technology.

title: Nvidia-backed Fireworks hits $17.5 billion valuation as companies pursue cheaper AI models
headline_summary: Fireworks hits $17.5B: cheaper AI inference gaining enterprise traction
blurb: Fireworks, an AI inference startup backed by Nvidia, reached a $17.5 billion valuation. The company previously depended heavily on Cursor for most of its revenue but has now diversified as enterprises seek cheaper AI model hosting and inference options. The valuation demonstrates strong market demand for cost-efficient AI infrastructure separate from large foundation model builders and established cloud giants.

title: The VC who backed Spotify, Revolut and Airbnb just closed a $300M defence fund
headline_summary: Lakestar closes $300M defence fund after backing Spotify, Revolut, Airbnb
blurb: Lakestar closed a $300M defence fund targeting distressed growth companies and M&A opportunities. The early-stage investor made its name backing Spotify, Revolut, and Airbnb from inception. The fund signals a strategic pivot among leading early-stage VCs: pulling back from aggressive new deployment and rotating capital toward defensive positioning, stabilizing portfolio companies, and facilitating advantageous M&A as the market environment cools.

title: Databricks raises at $188B as Coatue’s enterprise AI governance play outpaces the models underneath it
headline_summary: Databricks raises at $188B as Coatue bets on AI governance
blurb: Databricks closed a raise at $188B valuation, with Coatue leading the investment round. The Berkeley-founded data infrastructure company, now valued higher than most foundational AI labs, commercializes Apache Spark and manages enterprise data pipelines for AI workloads. This signals that governance and data access tooling commands premium multiples, showing the market prioritizes enterprise control and compliance over raw model capability.

title: Zalando joins Sereact's $116M Series B to accelerate AI-powered warehouse automation
headline_summary: Zalando backs warehouse robotics firm Sereact's $116M Series B
blurb: Sereact, a warehouse robotics startup, announced a $116M Series B with Zalando joining as a strategic investor. Zalando is Europe's largest fashion and lifestyle technology platform—its participation validates physical AI warehouse automation as a core enterprise category. For VCs, major retailers backing supply-chain robotics signals durable enterprise demand and marks the maturation of logistics automation as a sustained, fundable investment thesis.

title: Wonder raises $650M at $9B and Marc Lore, who oversaw $3.85B in exits at Amazon and Walmart, says an IPO is next
headline_summary: Wonder raises $650M at $9B; Lore eyes IPO next
blurb: Wonder raised $650M in new funding, closing at a $9B valuation. Marc Lore led the round and has a legendary track record of $3.85B in exits across Amazon and Walmart, including the high-profile Quidsi acquisition. Lore's public IPO timeline signals market confidence in his execution ability, and this round positions Wonder as a critical barometer for e-commerce momentum in 2026.

title: AI Pioneer Kai-Fu Lee’s Startup Targets Hong Kong IPO Next Year
headline_summary: 01.ai targets 2027 Hong Kong IPO, speeds up fundraising
blurb: 01.ai is raising capital before a planned Hong Kong IPO in 2027. Founded by computer scientist Kai-Fu Lee, the Chinese AI startup is accelerating its fundraising efforts ahead of the public market debut. This move signals significant momentum in Asian tech listings and reflects growing investor appetite for established AI founders navigating Hong Kong's regulatory pathways to major public exits.

title: Anthropic moves closer to mega-IPO as bankers line up investor meetings
headline_summary: Anthropic lines up investor meetings for potential October IPO
blurb: Anthropic is preparing for an IPO targeting October 2026, lining up investor meetings for a mega-offering. The AI startup behind Claude is racing to beat OpenAI and become the first major generative AI company to go public. The move signals strong institutional appetite for large-scale AI infrastructure, marking a major liquidity milestone for Anthropic's backers and the broader AI industry.
